skills.name: Programming Fundamentals
skills.description: Core programming knowledge including syntax, control flow, functions, OOP, error handling, and writing clean, readable code across one or more languages.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can write basic scripts, understand variables, loops, conditionals, and functions. Follows existing code patterns.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Writes well-structured, maintainable code using OOP principles. Understands design patterns, error handling, and testing basics.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Writes idiomatic, performant code across multiple languages. Deep understanding of language internals, memory models, and compilation/interpretation.

skills.name: Python Development
skills.description: Professional Python development for production systems, including frameworks, package management, type hints, testing, async programming, and Python-specific tooling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes functional Python scripts. Familiar with standard library, pip, and basic data structures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production services using frameworks (FastAPI, Django, Flask). Uses type hints, pytest, virtual environments, and async patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large Python codebases with advanced patterns (decorators, metaclasses, generators). Expert in performance profiling, C extensions, and Python internals.

skills.name: Backend Development
skills.description: Building server-side applications, APIs, and services. Includes web frameworks, database integration, authentication, caching, message queues, and production deployment.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build simple REST APIs with a framework. Understands request/response cycle, basic authentication, and database queries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and builds production-grade backend services with proper error handling, logging, caching, and security. Experienced with microservices patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex backend systems handling high throughput. Expert in distributed patterns, event-driven architectures, and performance optimization at scale.

skills.name: Frontend Development
skills.description: Building user-facing web applications using modern frameworks, component-based architecture, state management, responsive design, and browser APIs.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build basic web pages with HTML/CSS/JavaScript. Familiar with a modern framework (React, Vue, or Angular) at a basic level.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex UIs with component architecture, state management, routing, and API integration. Understands responsive design and accessibility.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large frontend applications with performance optimization, custom hooks/composables, design systems, and deep browser performance tuning.

skills.name: System Design Fundamentals
skills.description: Understanding of how software systems are structured, including client-server architecture, databases, APIs, caching, load balancing, and basic scalability concepts.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands client-server model, basic database design, and REST API structure.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design simple distributed systems with appropriate database choices, caching layers, and API contracts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs multi-component systems with proper trade-off analysis between consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.

skills.name: System Design & Architecture
skills.description: Advanced system design including distributed architectures, microservices, event-driven systems, database selection, caching strategies, load balancing, and scalability planning.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can articulate trade-offs between monolith and microservices. Understands basic distributed system challenges.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs scalable systems with appropriate patterns (event-driven, CQRS, pub/sub). Makes informed database and infrastructure choices.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests. Expert in consistency models, fault tolerance, and multi-region deployment strategies.

skills.name: System Architecture & Vision
skills.description: Defining long-term architectural vision for product domains, anticipating scaling bottlenecks, designing resilient distributed systems, and making strategic technology decisions that support 10x growth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can evaluate existing architectures and identify improvement areas. Understands architectural patterns and their trade-offs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Defines architectural roadmaps for product domains. Leads architectural migrations and modernization efforts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets enterprise-wide architectural vision. Anticipates scaling needs years ahead. Drives platform-level architectural decisions affecting multiple product lines.

skills.name: Distributed Systems
skills.description: Design and operation of systems distributed across multiple nodes, including consistency models, replication, partitioning, consensus protocols, and fault tolerance.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands CAP theorem, basic replication, and partitioning concepts. Can work with distributed databases and message queues.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs systems with appropriate consistency guarantees. Experienced with distributed caching, event streaming (Kafka), and service discovery.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects highly available, globally distributed systems. Expert in consensus protocols, eventual consistency patterns, and distributed transaction management.

skills.name: Git & Version Control
skills.description: Using Git for version control, branching strategies, pull requests, code review workflows, and collaborative development practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can clone, commit, push, pull, and create branches. Understands basic merge and pull request workflows.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses advanced branching strategies (GitFlow, trunk-based). Comfortable with rebasing, cherry-picking, and resolving complex merge conflicts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines Git workflows for teams. Expert in repository management, CI/CD integration, monorepo strategies, and Git internals.

skills.name: Debugging & Troubleshooting
skills.description: Systematic approaches to identifying and resolving software defects, including debugging tools, logging analysis, root cause analysis, and troubleshooting production issues.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use debuggers, print statements, and basic logging to identify simple bugs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Debugs complex multi-component issues using profiling tools, log aggregation, and systematic hypothesis testing.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert at diagnosing distributed system failures, race conditions, memory leaks, and production incidents under pressure.

skills.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
skills.description: Knowledge of fundamental data structures (arrays, trees, graphs, hash maps) and algorithms (sorting, searching, dynamic programming) including complexity analysis.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic data structures and their use cases. Can analyze simple time/space complexity.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Selects appropriate data structures for real-world problems. Implements efficient algorithms and understands common patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs custom data structures for performance-critical systems. Expert in algorithm optimization and computational complexity.

skills.name: Linux Fundamentals
skills.description: Working knowledge of Linux operating systems including command line, file systems, permissions, processes, package management, and basic system administration.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Navigates the file system, runs basic commands, understands permissions and processes.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Comfortable with shell scripting, service management (systemd), networking basics, and package management.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert in Linux internals, kernel concepts, performance tuning, and system administration at scale.

skills.name: Database Design & Management
skills.description: Design, query, and manage relational and NoSQL databases including schema design, indexing, query optimization, data modeling, and database selection for different use cases.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic SQL queries. Understands relational database concepts (tables, joins, indexes).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs efficient schemas, optimizes queries, and works with both SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis) databases.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects database solutions for high-scale systems. Expert in performance tuning, replication, sharding, and data modeling trade-offs.

skills.name: API Design & Development
skills.description: Designing and building APIs (REST, GraphQL, gRPC) with proper conventions, versioning, authentication, rate limiting, documentation, and error handling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build and consume basic REST APIs. Understands HTTP methods, status codes, and JSON payloads.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs well-structured APIs with proper versioning, authentication, pagination, and error handling. Experience with API documentation tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects API platforms with gRPC, GraphQL, and REST. Expert in API governance, backward compatibility, and high-performance API design.

skills.name: Testing & Quality Practices
skills.description: Writing and maintaining automated tests at multiple levels (unit, integration, end-to-end) and implementing quality practices including TDD, code coverage, and testing strategy.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic unit tests. Understands the testing pyramid and the purpose of different test types.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements comprehensive test suites with unit, integration, and e2e tests. Uses mocking, fixtures, and CI-integrated testing.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines organization-wide testing strategies. Expert in test architecture, performance testing, chaos engineering, and test infrastructure.

skills.name: Cloud Computing Fundamentals
skills.description: Basic understanding of cloud computing concepts including compute, storage, networking, and managed services across major cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure).

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands cloud vs. on-prem trade-offs. Can deploy basic services using a cloud provider's console or CLI.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Works with core cloud services (compute, storage, databases, networking). Understands IAM, VPCs, and basic security.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs cloud architectures with proper service selection, cost optimization, and security hardening.

skills.name: Cloud Platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure)
skills.description: Deep expertise with one or more major cloud platforms including advanced services, architecture patterns, cost optimization, and multi-cloud strategies.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Uses core services (EC2/GCE, S3/GCS, RDS) and understands cloud networking basics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs solutions using advanced services (Lambda, EKS, BigQuery, Pub/Sub). Implements proper IAM, monitoring, and cost management.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-scale cloud solutions. Expert in multi-account strategies, compliance, disaster recovery, and multi-cloud deployments.

skills.name: Performance Optimization
skills.description: Profiling, identifying, and resolving performance bottlenecks across systems including CPU, memory, network, and I/O. Covers application-level, database, and infrastructure optimization.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can identify obvious performance issues and apply basic optimization techniques (caching, query optimization).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses profiling tools to identify bottlenecks. Optimizes across application, database, and infrastructure layers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Drives systematic performance engineering across large-scale systems. Expert in latency analysis, resource optimization, and capacity planning.

skills.name: Technical Leadership
skills.description: Leading technical direction, making architectural decisions, driving engineering best practices, and influencing technical outcomes across teams without necessarily managing people directly.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Leads technical discussions within a team. Makes sound design decisions for features and components.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Drives technical direction for a product area. Sets engineering standards and leads cross-team technical initiatives.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines organization-wide technical vision. Influences architectural decisions across multiple product lines and mentors technical leaders.

skills.name: Mentoring & Coaching Engineers
skills.description: Developing other engineers through code reviews, pair programming, design reviews, career guidance, and knowledge sharing to elevate team capability and individual growth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides helpful feedback in code reviews. Occasionally pairs with junior engineers on specific tasks.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Actively mentors multiple engineers. Leads design reviews and introduces engineering patterns that improve team practices.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Develops senior engineers into tech leads. Creates mentorship programs and systematically elevates organizational engineering capability.

skills.name: Code Review Practices
skills.description: Conducting and participating in effective code reviews that improve code quality, share knowledge, and maintain engineering standards across the team.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides basic feedback on code correctness and style. Responds constructively to review feedback.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Reviews code for design, performance, testability, and maintainability. Provides actionable, educational feedback.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines code review standards and processes for the organization. Balances thoroughness with velocity and uses reviews as a mentorship tool.

skills.name: CI/CD Pipelines
skills.description: Building, maintaining, and optimizing continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines for automated testing, building, and deploying software.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands CI/CD concepts. Can work within existing pipelines and troubleshoot basic failures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds and maintains CI/CD pipelines with automated testing, linting, and deployment stages. Experience with GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or similar.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects CI/CD infrastructure for organizations. Expert in pipeline optimization, deployment strategies (blue-green, canary), and release management.

skills.name: Engineering People Management
skills.description: Managing engineering teams including hiring, performance reviews, career development, goal setting, and creating an environment where engineers thrive and deliver their best work.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages a small team (3-5 engineers). Conducts 1:1s, sets basic goals, and handles day-to-day team coordination.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a full team (5-10 engineers). Drives hiring, performance management, career development, and builds team culture.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages managers and large organizations (15+ engineers). Designs org structures, develops leadership pipeline, and drives engineering culture at scale.

skills.name: Engineering Hiring & Talent Acquisition
skills.description: Recruiting and evaluating engineering talent including defining role requirements, designing interview processes, conducting technical interviews, and building high-performing teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in interviews and provides structured candidate feedback. Helps define role requirements.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs interview processes for engineering roles. Conducts system design and coding interviews. Makes hiring decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds hiring strategies for scaling engineering organizations. Defines interview rubrics, sources talent, and creates employer brand.

skills.name: Performance Management
skills.description: Setting expectations, conducting performance reviews, providing feedback, coaching for improvement, and managing underperformance in engineering teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Sets clear expectations and provides regular feedback. Conducts basic performance reviews.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs performance frameworks with clear criteria. Handles difficult performance conversations and creates improvement plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds org-wide performance management systems. Develops calibration processes and links performance to career growth frameworks.

skills.name: Agile Methodology & Sprint Management
skills.description: Applying Agile/Scrum practices including sprint planning, backlog management, retrospectives, velocity tracking, and continuous delivery in engineering teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in sprint ceremonies. Understands user stories, estimation, and sprint workflow.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Facilitates sprint planning, retrospectives, and daily standups. Manages backlog and drives sprint delivery.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Adapts Agile practices to organizational needs. Coaches teams on Agile maturity and implements scaled Agile frameworks.

skills.name: Cross-Team Collaboration & Coordination
skills.description: Working effectively across engineering teams and with product, design, QA, and business stakeholders to drive aligned execution on shared goals and complex initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Collaborates effectively with immediate team and adjacent functions. Communicates status and blockers clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads cross-team initiatives and resolves inter-team dependencies. Aligns multiple stakeholders on technical decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Orchestrates complex, multi-team programs. Drives organizational alignment on strategic technical initiatives.

skills.name: Engineering Organization Leadership
skills.description: Leading engineering organizations at scale including organizational design, talent strategy, engineering culture, process excellence, and alignment between engineering execution and business goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Leads a single engineering team with clear goals and processes. Builds team culture and ensures delivery.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads multiple teams through managers. Designs org structures, drives process improvements, and aligns engineering with product strategy.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads entire engineering organizations. Defines engineering vision, drives major transformations, and represents engineering at the executive level.

skills.name: Engineering Organizational Design
skills.description: Designing engineering team structures, reporting lines, and collaboration models that optimize for delivery speed, quality, and engineer satisfaction at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands different team structures (feature teams, platform teams, squads). Can articulate trade-offs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs team structures for a product area. Balances team autonomy with organizational alignment.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs org structures for entire engineering functions. Expert in scaling team models, matrix organizations, and platform/product team dynamics.

skills.name: Executive Leadership & Strategy
skills.description: Operating at the executive level including setting technology vision, managing budgets, communicating with board/investors, making strategic trade-offs, and driving company-wide initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in executive discussions. Can articulate engineering strategy and trade-offs to senior leadership.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads executive-level initiatives. Manages budgets, drives strategic planning, and influences company direction.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Operates as a full executive leader. Drives company strategy, manages P&L, and represents the company externally.

skills.name: Talent Strategy & Workforce Planning
skills.description: Strategic planning of engineering workforce including headcount planning, skill gap analysis, succession planning, retention strategies, and building a leadership pipeline.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Identifies hiring needs and skill gaps within a team. Creates basic job descriptions and hiring plans.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops talent strategies for multiple teams. Plans succession, identifies high-potential engineers, and implements retention programs.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs company-wide engineering talent strategy. Builds employer brand, creates leadership development programs, and drives organizational growth.

skills.name: AI Strategy & Adoption
skills.description: Driving strategic adoption of AI across engineering workflows, product development, and organizational processes. Includes evaluating AI tools, defining AI-first engineering practices, and building AI capabilities into products.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Uses AI tools personally for development productivity. Advocates for AI adoption within the team.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Defines AI-first engineering practices for teams. Evaluates and integrates AI tools into development workflows. Drives AI feature integration into products.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets organization-wide AI strategy. Leads AI transformation initiatives, builds AI capabilities into the platform, and drives measurable impact from AI adoption.

skills.name: Stakeholder Management
skills.description: Managing relationships and communication with diverse stakeholders including product, sales, customers, and executive leadership to drive aligned decision-making and execution.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Communicates clearly with immediate stakeholders. Manages expectations on timelines and deliverables.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Navigates complex stakeholder dynamics across product, engineering, and business functions. Influences without authority.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages executive-level stakeholder relationships. Drives strategic alignment and represents engineering in company-wide decisions.

skills.name: Manual Testing
skills.description: Performing manual software testing including functional testing, regression testing, exploratory testing, and UI/UX validation to ensure software quality.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Executes test cases and reports bugs with clear reproduction steps. Performs basic functional and regression testing.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs test strategies and performs exploratory testing. Identifies edge cases and validates complex user flows across platforms.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads test planning for major releases. Expert in risk-based testing, cross-platform testing strategies, and quality assessment.

skills.name: Test Automation
skills.description: Building and maintaining automated test suites using frameworks like Playwright, Cypress, or Selenium for end-to-end, API, and regression testing.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic automated tests using an existing framework. Can maintain and update existing test suites.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and implements automated test suites with page objects, fixtures, and CI integration. Experienced with Playwright or Cypress.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects test automation frameworks from scratch. Expert in parallel execution, visual regression, and AI-assisted test generation.

skills.name: Test Planning & Strategy
skills.description: Creating comprehensive test plans, defining test scope, identifying risk areas, and establishing testing strategies that ensure coverage and quality for releases.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic test cases for assigned features. Understands test coverage concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive test plans with risk-based prioritization. Defines test scope and coverage for feature releases.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines org-wide testing strategies. Expert in shift-left testing, test architecture, and quality gate definition.

skills.name: Bug Tracking & Defect Management
skills.description: Identifying, documenting, tracking, and managing software defects through their lifecycle using bug tracking tools and clear communication practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Reports bugs with clear steps to reproduce, expected vs. actual behavior, and severity classification.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages defect lifecycle including prioritization, tracking trends, and coordinating fixes with developers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Analyzes defect patterns to drive process improvements. Builds defect tracking workflows and quality dashboards.

skills.name: API Testing
skills.description: Testing APIs for functionality, performance, security, and reliability using tools like Postman, automated test scripts, or API testing frameworks.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Tests API endpoints manually using Postman. Validates responses against expected outputs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Writes automated API test suites. Tests error handling, edge cases, authentication, and performance under load.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive API testing strategies including contract testing, load testing, and security testing.

skills.name: QA Methodology & Processes
skills.description: Knowledge of QA methodologies including Agile QA, shift-left testing, risk-based testing, release gating, and continuous quality improvement practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic QA processes and Agile QA workflow. Follows established testing procedures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements QA best practices within a team. Drives shift-left testing and integrates QA into the development lifecycle.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines QA strategy and methodology for an organization. Leads continuous quality improvement and introduces AI-assisted QA practices.

skills.name: Analytical Thinking
skills.description: Breaking down complex problems into components, analyzing root causes, evaluating evidence, and making logical, data-informed decisions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can break down simple problems and identify basic cause-effect relationships.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Analyzes complex multi-variable problems. Uses data and evidence to support conclusions and decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Drives strategic analysis of ambiguous, high-stakes problems. Builds analytical frameworks used by teams and organizations.

skills.name: Cloud Platforms for DevOps
skills.description: Deep operational expertise with cloud platforms focused on infrastructure management, networking, IAM, cost optimization, and service deployment at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages basic cloud resources. Understands IAM, VPCs, and core compute/storage services.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and manages cloud infrastructure including networking, multi-account strategies, and cost optimization.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-scale cloud environments. Expert in multi-cloud, compliance, disaster recovery, and infrastructure security.

skills.name: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
skills.description: Defining and managing infrastructure through code using tools like Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation, or AWS CDK for reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic Terraform/CloudFormation templates. Understands state management and resource definitions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds modular, reusable IaC with proper state management, environments, and CI/CD integration.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects IaC frameworks for organizations. Expert in multi-account provisioning, drift detection, and IaC governance.

skills.name: Containerization & Orchestration
skills.description: Working with container technologies (Docker) and orchestration platforms (Kubernetes) for packaging, deploying, and managing applications at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates Dockerfiles and runs containers. Understands basic Kubernetes concepts (pods, services, deployments).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages Kubernetes clusters with Helm charts, ConfigMaps, secrets, and networking. Implements deployment strategies.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects Kubernetes-based platforms. Expert in custom operators, multi-cluster management, service mesh, and Kubernetes security.

skills.name: Linux System Administration
skills.description: Advanced Linux system management including systemd, networking, storage, security hardening, performance tuning, and troubleshooting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages Linux services, users, and packages. Basic understanding of systemd and networking.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Administers production Linux systems. Experienced with networking configuration, storage management, and security hardening.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert in Linux internals, kernel tuning, performance analysis, and managing Linux at enterprise scale.

skills.name: Scripting & Automation
skills.description: Writing scripts in Python, Bash, or similar languages to automate operational tasks, infrastructure management, deployments, and repetitive workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes simple Bash/Python scripts for basic automation tasks.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds robust automation scripts with error handling, logging, and integration with APIs and cloud services.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive automation frameworks. Expert in building self-service tooling and intelligent automation systems.

skills.name: Monitoring & Observability
skills.description: Implementing monitoring, logging, alerting, and observability solutions to ensure system health, detect issues, and enable fast incident resolution.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Sets up basic monitoring dashboards and alerts. Understands metrics, logs, and traces.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs observability stacks with proper metric collection, log aggregation, distributed tracing, and alert tuning.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise observability platforms. Expert in SLI/SLO definition, AI-powered anomaly detection, and observability-driven development.

skills.name: Networking Fundamentals
skills.description: Understanding of network concepts including TCP/IP, DNS, load balancing, VPCs, firewalls, VPNs, and network security relevant to cloud and infrastructure engineering.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and basic network troubleshooting.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Configures VPCs, subnets, load balancers, and security groups. Understands network architectures for cloud environments.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs complex network architectures including multi-region, hybrid cloud, and secure tunnel configurations.

skills.name: Security Best Practices
skills.description: Implementing security practices across infrastructure and application layers including IAM, encryption, secrets management, vulnerability management, and compliance.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Follows security best practices for IAM, secrets, and basic encryption. Understands OWASP top 10.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements security controls across CI/CD, infrastructure, and applications. Experienced with secrets management and compliance frameworks.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs security architectures for organizations. Expert in DevSecOps, threat modeling, and security compliance at scale.

skills.name: Production Systems Operations
skills.description: Operating and maintaining production systems including deployment management, incident response, capacity planning, and ensuring system reliability and availability.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in on-call. Can follow runbooks and escalate issues appropriately.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages production deployments and incident response. Implements deployment strategies (blue-green, canary) and capacity planning.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs production operations practices for organizations. Expert in chaos engineering, SRE principles, and production reliability at scale.

skills.name: Incident Management & Response
skills.description: Managing production incidents from detection through resolution, including on-call practices, escalation procedures, communication, root cause analysis, and post-mortem processes.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Responds to incidents following established procedures. Documents incidents and participates in post-mortems.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads incident response. Conducts root cause analysis, writes post-mortems with actionable follow-ups, and improves incident processes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs incident management frameworks for organizations. Builds AI-powered incident detection and automated mitigation systems.

skills.name: Communication Skills
skills.description: Clear written and verbal communication for technical discussions, documentation, presentations, cross-team collaboration, and stakeholder updates.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Communicates clearly in team settings. Writes understandable documentation and bug reports.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Presents technical concepts to diverse audiences. Writes effective technical proposals, design docs, and status reports.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Communicates at the executive level. Influences organizational decisions through clear articulation of technical strategy and trade-offs.
